name: Banana
overview: Banana is a major fruit crop of India grown extensively for its energy-rich fruit, used both as a table fruit and in processing. It is a perennial herbaceous crop cultivated through tissue-cultured or sucker planting material, valued for year-round availability and high nutritional content.

soil.type: Deep, fertile loamy soil rich in organic matter
soil.drainage: Well-drained with good water-holding capacity

waterRequirement: 1800-2200 mm per crop cycle; drip irrigation recommended at 2-3 day intervals

yield.profitability: High profitability due to year-round demand and processing value

marketDemand: Consistently high demand across domestic and export markets; used fresh and in chips, flour, and fiber industries

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.purpose: Improves soil organic matter and water retention

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.purpose: Promotes vegetative growth and bunch development
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at 2, 4, 6 months after planting

fertilizers.name: Potassium (MOP)
fertilizers.purpose: Improves fruit quality and bunch weight
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split application during vegetative and bunching stage

majorDiseases.name: Panama Wilt (Fusarium wilt)
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellowing of older leaves, splitting of pseudostem, internal vascular discoloration
majorDiseases.management: Use resistant varieties, avoid waterlogging, soil drenching with Carbendazim, crop rotation

majorDiseases.name: Sigatoka Leaf Spot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Brown to black streaks on leaves leading to leaf drying
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb or Propiconazole, remove infected leaves, ensure proper spacing

majorDiseases.name: Bunchy Top Virus
majorDiseases.symptoms: Stunted growth, bunched narrow leaves at top
majorDiseases.management: Use virus-free planting material, control aphid vectors, uproot infected plants

pests.name: Banana Weevil Borer
pests.damage: Bores into pseudostem and rhizome causing plant collapse
pests.control: Field sanitation, pheromone traps, application of Chlorpyrifos around base

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Transmit bunchy top virus and suck plant sap
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid or neem-based insecticides

pests.name: Nematodes
pests.damage: Root damage leading to toppling of plants
pests.control: Soil application of Carbofuran, use of nematode-free suckers

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Deep ploughing followed by harrowing to achieve fine tilth; pits of 45x45x45 cm dug at recommended spacing and filled with FYM and topsoil mixture.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select disease-free tissue-cultured plantlets or healthy sword suckers from virus-free mother plants, ensuring uniform growth and high yield potential.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Plant suckers or tissue-culture plants in pits at spacing of 1.8x1.8 m to 2x2 m depending on variety, during onset of monsoon or post-monsoon period.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Maintain consistent soil moisture through drip irrigation; banana is highly sensitive to water stress especially during flowering and bunch formation.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply recommended NPK in split doses along with micronutrients like zinc and boron; fertigation through drip system improves nutrient use efficiency.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Practice regular interculturing and mulching to suppress weeds; remove excess suckers to maintain single stem per mat.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor regularly for weevils, nematodes and fungal diseases; adopt integrated pest management combining cultural, biological and chemical control.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest bunches when fingers turn plump and angular at 75% maturity, typically 11-13 months after planting, using sharp tools to avoid bruising.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Handle bunches carefully to avoid mechanical injury, ripen using controlled ethylene treatment, grade and pack in ventilated cartons for transport.

advantages: Suitable for intercropping in early stages
storageGuidelines: Store green bananas at 13-14°C with 85-90% relative humidity; avoid storage below 12°C to prevent chilling injury; use controlled ripening chambers for uniform color development.

name: Coconut
overview: Coconut is a versatile plantation crop grown predominantly in coastal regions of India, valued for its multiple products including copra, oil, fiber, and tender nut water. It is a long-duration perennial palm crop with significant commercial and subsistence value.
season: Year-round; planting done at onset of monsoon
cropDuration: 6-8 years to bear fruit, productive for 60+ years

soil.type: Sandy loam to laterite soil with good aeration
soil.drainage: Well-drained, deep soil profile

waterRequirement: 40-50 litres per tree per week during dry periods; basin irrigation common

yield.profitability: Stable long-term income with low annual input cost once established

marketDemand: Strong demand for copra, coconut oil, desiccated coconut, tender coconut water, and coir products

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.applicationStage: Annual basal application around tree basin

fertilizers.name: Potassium (MOP)
fertilizers.purpose: Improves nut size and copra content
fertilizers.applicationStage: Applied in two split doses annually

majorDiseases.name: Bud Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Rotting of central spindle leaves with foul smell
majorDiseases.management: Remove affected tissue, apply Bordeaux paste, drench with Metalaxyl

majorDiseases.name: Root Wilt Disease
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellowing of leaflets, reduced nut size, bending of leaves
majorDiseases.management: Balanced fertilization, removal of severely affected palms, resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Leaf Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Brown spots on leaflets coalescing into blighted patches
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, improve drainage and aeration

pests.name: Rhinoceros Beetle
pests.damage: Bores into crown causing damage to growing point
pests.control: Use of pheromone traps, insertion of naphthalene balls in crown

pests.name: Red Palm Weevil
pests.damage: Larvae bore into trunk causing palm collapse
pests.control: Avoid injury to trunk, apply Chlorpyrifos in leaf axils, pheromone trapping

pests.name: Eriophyid Mite
pests.damage: Causes triangular patches and shedding of immature nuts
pests.control: Spray neem oil or sulphur-based acaricides

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Dig pits of 1x1x1 m at spacing of 7.5x7.5 m; fill with topsoil, FYM, and sand mixture for proper drainage and root establishment.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select disease-free, high-yielding hybrid or local tall/dwarf variety seedlings aged 9-12 months with at least 6 leaves and collar girth.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Plant seedlings at the onset of monsoon, ensuring the collar region remains slightly above ground level to prevent collar rot.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide basin irrigation during dry months, especially in first 3 years of establishment; mature palms need irrigation during summer.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply organic manure and balanced NPK fertilizers annually in basins; include micronutrients like magnesium and boron for healthy crown growth.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Maintain weed-free basins through regular hoeing; grow leguminous cover crops to suppress weeds and fix nitrogen.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Regularly inspect crown and trunk for beetle and weevil infestation; remove and destroy bud rot affected tissue promptly.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest mature nuts (11-12 months after pollination) by climbing or using mechanical harvesters; tender nuts harvested at 7-8 months for water.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested nuts for copra extraction, dehusk for coir processing, and grade nuts based on size and maturity for market sale.

advantages: Suitable for coastal and inland regions
challenges: Vulnerable to cyclones and strong winds
storageGuidelines: Store mature dried nuts in well-ventilated areas; copra should be dried to below 6% moisture and stored in jute bags to prevent fungal contamination.

name: Rice
overview: Rice is the staple food crop of India and the most important Kharif cereal, grown extensively in irrigated, rainfed, and deepwater ecosystems. It is highly water-intensive and forms the backbone of food security in eastern and southern India.

soil.type: Clayey loam to clay soil with high water retention
soil.drainage: Poorly drained, puddled fields preferred

waterRequirement: 1200-1500 mm; continuous standing water of 5-10 cm during vegetative stage

yield.profitability: Moderate profitability, supported by MSP and government procurement

marketDemand: Stable and assured demand through government procurement and strong domestic consumption; export demand for basmati varieties

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at transplanting, tillering, and panicle initiation

fertilizers.name: Potassium (MOP)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split at basal and panicle initiation stage

fertilizers.name: Zinc Sulphate
fertilizers.purpose: Corrects zinc deficiency common in puddled soils

majorDiseases.name: Blast Disease
majorDiseases.symptoms: Spindle-shaped lesions on leaves with grey centers, neck rot
majorDiseases.management: Spray Tricyclazole, use resistant varieties, avoid excess nitrogen

majorDiseases.name: Bacterial Leaf Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Water-soaked lesions turning yellow and drying from leaf tip
majorDiseases.management: Use resistant varieties, avoid excess nitrogen, spray Copper oxychloride

majorDiseases.name: Sheath Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Oval lesions on leaf sheath near water line
majorDiseases.management: Spray Validamycin, maintain proper spacing for aeration

pests.name: Stem Borer
pests.damage: Causes deadheart in vegetative stage and whiteheads in reproductive stage
pests.control: Use pheromone traps, apply Cartap hydrochloride granules

pests.name: Brown Planthopper
pests.damage: Sucks sap causing hopperburn and transmits viral diseases
pests.control: Avoid excess nitrogen, spray Imidacloprid, maintain field hygiene

pests.name: Gall Midge
pests.damage: Forms silver shoot galls preventing panicle formation
pests.control: Use resistant varieties, apply systemic insecticides

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field 2-3 times, puddle with standing water to create soft mud and level the field to ensure uniform water distribution.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified high-yielding or hybrid seeds suited to local conditions; treat seeds with fungicide before sowing to prevent seed-borne diseases.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Raise nursery for 20-25 days, then transplant seedlings at 2-3 per hill with spacing of 20x15 cm; direct seeding also practiced in some regions.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Maintain 5-10 cm standing water throughout vegetative stage; drain field 7-10 days before harvest to facilitate easy harvesting.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply fertilizers in split doses synchronized with critical growth stages; incorporate green manure or FYM before transplanting for organic matter.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Control weeds through pre-emergence herbicides like Butachlor followed by manual weeding or use of weeders at 20-25 days after transplanting.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for stem borer, planthoppers and blast disease regularly; adopt IPM practices including light traps and need-based pesticide application.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when 80-85% of grains turn golden yellow and moisture content drops to 20-22%, using sickles or combine harvesters.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Thresh promptly, dry grains to 12-14% moisture, clean and store in moisture-proof bags to prevent fungal infestation and insect damage.

advantages: High calorie staple with strong demand
Multiple variety options for diverse agro-climates
challenges: Vulnerable to pest and disease outbreaks
intercroppingOptions: Not commonly intercropped; sometimes rotated with pulses or fish (rice-fish culture)
storageGuidelines: Dry paddy to 12-14% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry, well-ventilated godowns using moisture-proof packaging to prevent weevil infestation.

name: Maize
overview: Maize is a versatile cereal crop grown across Kharif, Rabi, and spring seasons in India, used for food, fodder, and industrial purposes including starch and ethanol production. It adapts well to diverse agro-climatic zones.

soil.type: Well-drained loamy to sandy loam soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential, intolerant to waterlogging

waterRequirement: 500-800 mm; critical stages at knee-high, tasseling and grain filling

yield.profitability: Good profitability due to dual use as food and fodder/industrial crop

marketDemand: Rising demand from poultry feed, starch, and ethanol industries alongside traditional food use

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at sowing, knee-high, and tasseling stage

fertilizers.name: Zinc Sulphate
fertilizers.purpose: Prevents zinc deficiency causing white bud

majorDiseases.name: Turcicum Leaf Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Long elliptical grey-green lesions on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, use resistant hybrids, crop rotation

majorDiseases.name: Maize Stalk Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Softening and rotting of lower stalk causing lodging
majorDiseases.management: Avoid waterlogging, balanced potassium application, resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Downy Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: Chlorotic streaking and stunted growth
majorDiseases.management: Seed treatment with Metalaxyl, remove infected plants

pests.name: Fall Armyworm
pests.damage: Feeds on whorl and leaves causing severe defoliation
pests.control: Spray Emamectin benzoate, use pheromone traps, early sowing

pests.name: Stem Borer
pests.damage: Bores into stem causing deadheart
pests.control: Apply Carbofuran granules in whorl, use resistant hybrids

pests.name: Shoot Fly
pests.damage: Causes wilting of central shoot in seedling stage
pests.control: Seed treatment with Imidacloprid, timely sowing

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field deeply followed by 2-3 harrowings to achieve fine tilth; form ridges and furrows for proper drainage and irrigation.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified hybrid seeds suited to season and region with good germination percentage; treat seeds with fungicide and insecticide.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 2-3 cm depth with row spacing of 60-75 cm and plant spacing of 20-25 cm, either by dibbling or seed drill.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Irrigate at critical growth stages including knee-high stage, tasseling, silking and grain filling; avoid waterlogging at any stage.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply fertilizers in split doses with nitrogen top-dressed at knee-high and tasseling stages; incorporate organic manure before sowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct first weeding at 20-25 days after sowing followed by earthing up; pre-emergence herbicide like Atrazine can be used.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Scout fields regularly for fall armyworm and stem borer; apply recommended insecticides at early larval stages for effective control.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when husk turns brown and kernels harden, typically 90-110 days after sowing; field dry cobs before shelling.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Shell, dry grains to 12-14% moisture, clean and store in moisture-proof containers to prevent weevil and fungal damage.

advantages: Dual-purpose use as food and feed
challenges: Price volatility based on feed industry demand
High input cost for hybrid seeds
storageGuidelines: Dry maize grains to below 14% moisture; store in hermetically sealed bags or silos with regular fumigation to prevent weevil infestation.

name: Sugarcane
overview: Sugarcane is a major commercial cash crop of India and the primary raw material for sugar and jaggery production. It is a long-duration crop requiring intensive water and nutrient management, supporting a significant agro-industrial economy.

soil.type: Deep fertile loam to clay loam soil
soil.drainage: Well-drained with good moisture retention

waterRequirement: 1800-2500 mm; irrigation every 7-10 days depending on season

yield.profitability: High profitability with assured procurement by sugar mills

marketDemand: Strong and consistent demand from sugar mills, jaggery units, and ethanol blending programs

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at planting, tillering, and grand growth stage

fertilizers.name: Potassium (MOP)
fertilizers.purpose: Improves sucrose content and disease resistance
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split application at tillering and grand growth stage

majorDiseases.name: Red Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Reddening of internal tissue with white patches, hollow sound on tapping
majorDiseases.management: Use resistant varieties, hot water treatment of setts, avoid waterlogging

majorDiseases.name: Smut
majorDiseases.symptoms: Black whip-like structure emerging from growing point
majorDiseases.management: Remove and burn infected clumps, use disease-free setts, resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Wilt
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellowing and drying of leaves, shrinking of internodes
majorDiseases.management: Field sanitation, balanced fertilization, resistant varieties

pests.name: Early Shoot Borer
pests.damage: Bores into young shoots causing deadheart
pests.control: Apply Chlorpyrifos in soil, remove and destroy affected shoots

pests.name: Top Borer
pests.damage: Damages growing point causing bunchy top appearance
pests.control: Release Trichogramma parasitoids, apply systemic insecticides

pests.name: Pyrilla (Leafhopper)
pests.damage: Sucks sap causing yellowing and reduced sugar content
pests.control: Release Epiricania parasitoid, spray Imidacloprid if severe

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Deep plough field followed by harrowing and leveling; form furrows at spacing of 90-120 cm for planting setts.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select healthy 3-budded setts from 8-10 month old disease-free seed crop, treating with fungicide and hot water before planting.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Plant setts end-to-end in furrows at recommended spacing, covering lightly with soil during February-March or October-November.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Irrigate immediately after planting and maintain regular irrigation cycles, increasing frequency during grand growth phase.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply fertilizers in split doses synchronized with tillering and grand growth stages; earth up after each fertilizer application.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct manual or mechanical weeding at 30, 60 and 90 days; use pre-emergence herbicides like Atrazine for early weed control.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Regularly scout for borers and red rot symptoms; remove and destroy affected clumps, use biological control agents where possible.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest at 10-12 months when cane reaches optimum sucrose content, cutting close to ground level using sickles or harvesters.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Transport cane to mill within 24-48 hours of harvest to minimize sucrose loss; avoid prolonged field storage after cutting.

advantages: High biomass and by-product value (bagasse, molasses)
challenges: Long crop duration ties up land
storageGuidelines: Sugarcane should be processed within 24-48 hours of harvest as sucrose content deteriorates rapidly; not suitable for long-term storage in raw form.

name: Papaya
overview: Papaya is a fast-growing fruit crop valued for its short gestation period, continuous fruiting, and high nutritional value. It is widely cultivated for fresh fruit consumption, papain extraction, and processing into juices and candies.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam rich in organic matter
soil.drainage: Excellent drainage essential, sensitive to waterlogging

waterRequirement: 1200-1500 mm annually; frequent light irrigation preferred

yield.profitability: High profitability due to quick returns and continuous harvest

marketDemand: High and steady demand for fresh fruit consumption and processing industry (papain, juices)

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.applicationStage: Basal application at planting and annually thereafter

majorDiseases.name: Papaya Ringspot Virus
majorDiseases.symptoms: Ring-shaped spots on fruit, mosaic mottling on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Use virus-free seedlings, control aphid vectors, remove infected plants

majorDiseases.name: Powdery Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves and young fruits
majorDiseases.management: Spray Wettable sulphur or Dinocap, improve air circulation

majorDiseases.name: Foot Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Rotting at collar region leading to plant collapse
majorDiseases.management: Avoid waterlogging, drench with Metalaxyl, ensure raised bed planting

pests.name: Papaya Mealybug
pests.damage: Sucks sap causing curling of leaves and sooty mold
pests.control: Release Acerophagus papayae parasitoid, spray neem oil

pests.name: Fruit Fly
pests.damage: Larvae infest ripening fruit causing rotting
pests.control: Use methyl eugenol traps, bag fruits, field sanitation

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Transmit ringspot virus and cause leaf curling
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid, remove weed hosts

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Prepare land with deep ploughing and dig pits of 60x60x60 cm at 1.8x1.8 m spacing, filling with FYM and topsoil mixture.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select healthy seeds from high-yielding hermaphrodite or dioecious varieties; raise seedlings in polybags under nursery conditions.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Transplant 45-60 day old seedlings into prepared pits during early morning or evening, maintaining adequate spacing between plants.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide regular light irrigation, especially during flowering and fruiting; install drip irrigation for water-use efficiency.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply balanced fertilizers monthly in split doses, supplemented with micronutrients like boron and zinc for fruit quality.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Maintain weed-free basins through mulching and shallow hoeing; avoid deep cultivation near root zone to prevent root damage.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for mealybug, fruit fly and viral symptoms regularly; remove and destroy infected plants to prevent disease spread.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest fruits when they show slight yellowing at the apex (color break stage), typically 9-12 months after planting.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Handle fruits gently to avoid latex staining and bruising; grade by size and ripeness, pack in ventilated cartons for transport.

challenges: Sensitive to waterlogging and strong winds
storageGuidelines: Store mature green fruits at 10-13°C with 85-90% humidity for transport; ripe fruits should be consumed or processed within a few days at room temperature.

name: Jowar
overview: Jowar, or sorghum, is a drought-tolerant cereal crop widely grown in semi-arid regions of India, valued as both a staple food grain and quality fodder crop. It plays a crucial role in dryland farming systems.

soil.type: Medium to deep black soil (vertisols)
soil.drainage: Moderate drainage, tolerates mild moisture stress

climate.humidity: Low to moderate humidity, drought tolerant

waterRequirement: 350-500 mm; relatively low water requirement compared to other cereals

yield.perHectare: 2.5-4 tonnes grain plus substantial fodder yield
yield.profitability: Moderate profitability with strong fodder value addition

marketDemand: Steady demand for food grain in semi-arid regions and growing demand for poultry feed and fodder

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at sowing and 30 days after sowing

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.applicationStage: Applied before sowing during land preparation

majorDiseases.name: Grain Smut
majorDiseases.symptoms: Grains replaced by black sooty spore masses
majorDiseases.management: Seed treatment with Carbendazim, use resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Charcoal Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Greying and shredding of stalk tissue, lodging
majorDiseases.management: Avoid moisture stress at grain filling, balanced fertilization

majorDiseases.name: Anthracnose
majorDiseases.symptoms: Red to brown lesions on leaves and stalk
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, crop rotation, resistant varieties

pests.name: Shoot Fly
pests.damage: Causes deadheart in seedling stage
pests.control: Seed treatment with Imidacloprid, timely sowing with onset of rains

pests.name: Stem Borer
pests.damage: Bores into stem causing deadheart and reduced yield
pests.control: Apply Carbofuran granules, remove and destroy affected plants

pests.name: Earhead Bug
pests.damage: Sucks grain sap causing chaffy grains
pests.control: Spray Malathion at ear emergence stage

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field once or twice during pre-monsoon period to conserve moisture; harrow to break clods and create fine seedbed.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified hybrid or improved variety seeds with high germination rate; treat seeds with fungicide and insecticide before sowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 3-4 cm depth with row spacing of 45 cm and plant spacing of 12-15 cm using seed drill or dibbling method.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Rely primarily on rainfall in rainfed systems; provide supplemental irrigation at critical stages like flowering in irrigated areas.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply basal fertilizers at sowing followed by top dressing of nitrogen at 30 days after sowing for optimal vegetative growth.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct first weeding at 15-20 days after sowing followed by second weeding at 30-35 days; thinning to maintain optimal plant population.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for shoot fly during early stages and stem borer later; adopt timely sowing as primary cultural control measure.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when grains harden and moisture content drops to around 20%, typically 100-120 days after sowing, using sickles.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested earheads in sun, thresh to separate grains, clean and store at 12% moisture in jute bags or bins.

challenges: Lower market price compared to rice/wheat
Susceptible to bird damage at grain filling
storageGuidelines: Dry grains to 12% moisture before storage; store in airtight containers or gunny bags with neem leaves to prevent weevil infestation.

name: Bajra
overview: Bajra, or pearl millet, is a highly drought-resistant cereal crop predominantly grown in arid and semi-arid regions of India, serving as a vital food and fodder source in low-rainfall zones.

soil.type: Sandy to sandy loam soil
soil.drainage: Well-drained, tolerates low fertility and moisture stress

waterRequirement: 250-400 mm; among the lowest water requirements of cereal crops

yield.perHectare: 2-3.5 tonnes grain with good fodder yield
yield.profitability: Moderate profitability, valuable in low-input dryland systems

marketDemand: Growing demand due to nutritional millet promotion and poultry/cattle feed industry

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split dose at sowing and 25-30 days after sowing

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.purpose: Improves sandy soil fertility and moisture retention

fertilizers.name: Zinc Sulphate
fertilizers.purpose: Corrects zinc deficiency in sandy soils

majorDiseases.name: Downy Mildew (Green Ear)
majorDiseases.symptoms: Conversion of ear into leafy green structure, chlorosis on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Seed treatment with Metalaxyl, use resistant hybrids, remove infected plants

majorDiseases.name: Ergot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Honeydew secretion on earheads followed by hardened sclerotia
majorDiseases.management: Avoid excess nitrogen, use resistant varieties, seed treatment

majorDiseases.name: Smut
majorDiseases.symptoms: Grain replaced by smut sori containing black spore mass
majorDiseases.management: Field sanitation, resistant varieties, timely sowing

pests.name: Shoot Fly
pests.damage: Causes deadheart in seedling stage
pests.control: Timely sowing with monsoon onset, seed treatment with Imidacloprid

pests.name: Stem Borer
pests.damage: Larvae bore into stem reducing plant vigor
pests.control: Apply Carbofuran granules in whorl region

pests.name: Earhead Caterpillar
pests.damage: Feeds on developing grains in earhead
pests.control: Spray Quinalphos at ear emergence

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field once with onset of pre-monsoon showers; harrow lightly to prepare a fine seedbed suited to sandy soils.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select drought-tolerant hybrid or composite varieties with proven performance in local rainfall conditions; treat seeds with fungicide.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 2-3 cm depth with row spacing of 45-50 cm using seed drill, ensuring optimal plant population through proper thinning.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Largely rainfed cultivation; provide one or two supplemental irrigations at flowering and grain filling stage if available.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply basal phosphorus and half nitrogen at sowing, top-dress remaining nitrogen at 25-30 days after sowing during tillering.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Carry out first weeding at 15-20 days after sowing; thinning of excess seedlings to maintain recommended plant spacing.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Scout for shoot fly in early stage and downy mildew during humid conditions; rogue out infected plants showing green ear symptoms.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest earheads when grains harden and turn grey, around 75-90 days after sowing, by cutting panicles or whole plants.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested earheads thoroughly, thresh to separate grain, winnow and store at safe moisture levels in clean containers.

challenges: Susceptible to downy mildew in humid conditions
storageGuidelines: Dry grains to below 12% moisture; store in airtight bins or jute bags treated with neem-based protectants to prevent storage pest damage.

name: Groundnut
overview: Groundnut, also known as peanut, is a major oilseed and food legume crop of India, cultivated for edible oil extraction, confectionery use, and as a nitrogen-fixing legume beneficial for soil health.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential for pod development

waterRequirement: 500-600 mm; critical irrigation needed at pegging and pod development stages

yield.profitability: Good profitability driven by edible oil and export demand

marketDemand: Strong demand from edible oil industry, confectionery sector, and export markets

fertilizers.name: Gypsum (Calcium Sulphate)
fertilizers.purpose: Improves pod filling and kernel development

majorDiseases.name: Tikka Leaf Spot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Circular brown to black spots on leaves with yellow halo
majorDiseases.management: Spray Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb, use resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Collar Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Rotting at collar region with white fungal growth
majorDiseases.management: Seed treatment with Carbendazim, avoid waterlogging

majorDiseases.name: Bud Necrosis Virus
majorDiseases.symptoms: Necrotic streaks and bushy stunted growth
majorDiseases.management: Control thrips vector, use resistant varieties, remove infected plants

pests.name: Leaf Miner
pests.damage: Mines into leaf tissue causing serpentine trails and defoliation
pests.control: Spray Quinalphos, remove crop residue after harvest

pests.name: Thrips
pests.damage: Sucks sap and transmits bud necrosis virus
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid, use reflective mulch

pests.name: White Grub
pests.damage: Feeds on roots and pods causing wilting
pests.control: Soil application of Chlorpyrifos, deep summer ploughing

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field deeply to break hardpan and improve aeration for pod development; harrow to achieve fine, friable tilth.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select bold, disease-free pods of recommended variety; shell carefully to avoid kernel damage, treat with fungicide before sowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 3-5 cm depth with spacing of 30x10 cm for bunch types and 45x15 cm for spreading types, using seed drill or dibbling.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide irrigation at flowering, pegging, and pod development stages; avoid water stress during pod filling to ensure good yield.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and gypsum as basal dose; gypsum application at pegging stage significantly improves pod filling and kernel quality.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct early weeding at 20-25 days after sowing; earthing up at flowering stage to facilitate peg penetration into soil.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for leaf spot diseases and thrips regularly; adopt integrated pest management with biological and chemical control measures.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when leaves turn yellow and pods show characteristic net pattern, typically 100-130 days after sowing, by uprooting plants.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested plants in field, strip pods, further dry to 8-9% moisture, and store in well-ventilated, moisture-proof containers.

challenges: Susceptible to aflatoxin contamination if improperly dried
Price volatility with international oil markets
storageGuidelines: Dry pods to 8-9% kernel moisture before storage to prevent aflatoxin development; store in cool, dry, well-ventilated conditions away from moisture.

name: Cotton
overview: Cotton is India's most important fiber crop and a key raw material for the textile industry. It is predominantly grown as a Kharif crop with Bt hybrid varieties dominating cultivation across rainfed and irrigated tracts.

soil.type: Deep black cotton soil (regur) or alluvial soil
soil.drainage: Well-drained, moisture-retentive deep soils

climate.humidity: Moderate humidity, clear sky needed at boll opening

waterRequirement: 700-1200 mm; critical stages at squaring, flowering and boll development

yield.profitability: High profitability with Bt hybrids but input-intensive

marketDemand: Strong and consistent demand from domestic textile mills and export markets for raw cotton and yarn

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at sowing, squaring, and flowering

fertilizers.name: Potassium (MOP)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split application at squaring and boll formation

fertilizers.name: Magnesium Sulphate
fertilizers.purpose: Prevents reddening of leaves and improves photosynthesis

majorDiseases.name: Bacterial Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Angular water-soaked lesions on leaves turning brown
majorDiseases.management: Use resistant varieties, seed treatment with Streptocycline, avoid overhead irrigation

majorDiseases.name: Fusarium Wilt
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of plants, vascular browning
majorDiseases.management: Crop rotation, resistant varieties, soil drenching with Carbendazim

majorDiseases.name: Grey Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: Grey powdery patches on lower leaf surface
majorDiseases.management: Spray Wettable sulphur, ensure proper spacing

pests.name: Pink Bollworm
pests.damage: Bores into bolls causing rosette flowers and damaged lint
pests.control: Use pheromone traps, timely destruction of crop residue, Bt cotton with refuge

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.damage: Sucks sap and transmits leaf curl virus
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid, use yellow sticky traps

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Causes curling of leaves and honeydew secretion leading to sooty mold
pests.control: Spray Thiamethoxam, conserve natural predators

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Deep plough field in summer to expose soil to sunlight and reduce pest carryover; harrow to prepare fine, weed-free seedbed.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified Bt hybrid seeds suited to region with built-in bollworm resistance; treat seeds with recommended fungicide.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 4-5 cm depth with spacing of 90-120 cm between rows and 45-60 cm between plants, maintaining recommended population density.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide irrigation at critical stages including squaring, flowering, and boll development; avoid moisture stress during boll filling.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply fertilizers in split doses synchronized with vegetative, squaring and flowering stages; foliar nutrition during boll development.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct timely weeding at 20-25 and 45 days after sowing; use pre-emergence herbicide followed by inter-cultivation.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for pink bollworm and whitefly through regular field scouting and pheromone traps; follow refuge crop guidelines for Bt cotton.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest bolls in 3-4 pickings as they mature and open, typically starting 150-160 days after sowing, picking only opened bolls.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry picked kapas in sun, remove trash and contamination, store in clean, dry conditions before ginning to maintain fiber quality.

challenges: Pink bollworm resistance to Bt technology
storageGuidelines: Store picked cotton in dry, well-ventilated godowns away from moisture to prevent fiber discoloration; avoid contamination with foreign matter before ginning.

name: Tomato
overview: Tomato is one of the most widely cultivated vegetable crops in India, grown for fresh consumption and processing into sauces, ketchup, and paste. It is a high-value crop with year-round cultivation potential across diverse climatic zones.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil rich in organic matter
soil.drainage: Good drainage critical to prevent root diseases

climate.humidity: Moderate humidity; high humidity favors disease

waterRequirement: 600-800 mm; drip irrigation recommended for water efficiency and disease control

yield.profitability: High profitability but with significant price volatility

marketDemand: Very high demand across fresh and processing segments; prices highly volatile based on seasonal supply

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at transplanting, flowering, and fruiting

majorDiseases.name: Early Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Concentric ring spots on lower leaves leading to defoliation
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, crop rotation, remove infected debris

majorDiseases.name: Late Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves turning brown with white mold underneath
majorDiseases.management: Spray Metalaxyl + Mancozeb, avoid overhead irrigation

majorDiseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
majorDiseases.symptoms: Sudden wilting of plants despite adequate moisture
majorDiseases.management: Crop rotation, resistant varieties, soil solarization

pests.name: Fruit Borer
pests.damage: Bores into fruits causing rotting and yield loss
pests.control: Use pheromone traps, spray Spinosad, install bird perches

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.damage: Transmits leaf curl virus and causes sooty mold
pests.control: Yellow sticky traps, spray Imidacloprid, use insect-proof nursery nets

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Sucks sap causing curling and stunted growth
pests.control: Spray neem oil or Thiamethoxam

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Prepare raised beds after deep ploughing and incorporation of FYM; ensure proper drainage channels to avoid waterlogging.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select hybrid or improved variety seeds with resistance to wilt and viral diseases; treat seeds with fungicide before sowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Raise seedlings in nursery for 25-30 days, then transplant at spacing of 60x45 cm during evening hours to reduce transplant shock.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Install drip irrigation for efficient water use; irrigate at regular intervals avoiding water stress during flowering and fruiting.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply fertilizers in split doses through fertigation or soil application; supplement with calcium and micronutrients to prevent disorders.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Practice mulching with black polythene or organic mulch to suppress weeds and conserve soil moisture around root zone.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Stake plants for support, monitor regularly for fruit borer and viral diseases, adopt IPM with traps and need-based spraying.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest fruits at appropriate maturity stage (green, breaker, or red ripe) depending on market distance, typically starting 60-70 days after transplanting.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Sort and grade fruits by size and color, pack in ventilated crates, and transport quickly to minimize post-harvest losses.

advantages: Suitable for both fresh and processing markets
Adaptable to various growing systems including protected cultivation
challenges: Susceptible to viral and fungal diseases
storageGuidelines: Store mature green tomatoes at 12-15°C with 85-90% humidity for extended shelf life; ripe tomatoes should be marketed quickly due to high perishability.

name: Onion
overview: Onion is a critical commercial vegetable crop of India, cultivated extensively for both domestic consumption and export, particularly from Maharashtra. It is a bulb crop sensitive to day length and storage conditions.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential, sensitive to waterlogging

climate.humidity: Low humidity favorable at bulb maturity

waterRequirement: 350-600 mm; stop irrigation 2-3 weeks before harvest for better storage

yield.profitability: High profitability potential but significant price volatility

marketDemand: Major export commodity and domestic staple with frequent price fluctuations based on supply conditions

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.purpose: Improves soil structure for bulb development

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at transplanting and 30-45 days after

majorDiseases.name: Purple Blotch
majorDiseases.symptoms: Purple to brown lesions on leaves with concentric rings
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, avoid overhead irrigation, crop rotation

majorDiseases.name: Basal Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellowing of leaf tips progressing to base rot
majorDiseases.management: Use disease-free sets, avoid waterlogging, seed treatment with fungicide

majorDiseases.name: Downy Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: Pale yellow patches on leaves with grey fuzzy growth
majorDiseases.management: Spray Metalaxyl, ensure proper field drainage

pests.name: Thrips
pests.damage: Scrapes leaf surface causing silvery streaks and stunted growth
pests.control: Spray Fipronil or Imidacloprid, use blue sticky traps

pests.name: Onion Maggot
pests.damage: Larvae feed on bulb causing rotting
pests.control: Soil application of Chlorpyrifos, crop rotation

pests.name: Cutworm
pests.damage: Cuts seedlings at base during night
pests.control: Apply Chlorpyrifos dust around plant base

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Prepare fine, well-pulverized seedbed through repeated ploughing; form raised beds for nursery and ridges for main field planting.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified disease-free seeds of suitable variety based on season; treat seeds with Thiram before sowing in nursery beds.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Raise seedlings in nursery for 6-8 weeks, then transplant at spacing of 15x10 cm into main field during evening hours.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Irrigate immediately after transplanting and maintain regular shallow irrigation; withhold irrigation 2-3 weeks before harvest.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply fertilizers in split doses with emphasis on phosphorus and sulphur for bulb development; avoid excess nitrogen near maturity.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct regular shallow weeding to avoid damaging shallow root system; mulching helps in moisture conservation and weed control.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for thrips and purple blotch disease regularly; adopt IPM practices with sticky traps and timely fungicide application.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when neck of bulb softens and leaves begin to fall over, typically 100-150 days after transplanting depending on season.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Cure harvested bulbs in field for 3-4 days, then in shade for 10-15 days; cut tops, grade by size, and store in well-ventilated structures.

advantages: Good storage life with proper curing
challenges: Sensitive to excess rainfall at harvest
storageGuidelines: Cure bulbs properly before storage; store in well-ventilated structures with low humidity at 25-30°C, avoiding moisture to prevent rotting and sprouting.

name: Mango
overview: Mango is regarded as the king of fruits in India and is the most widely cultivated fruit crop, valued for fresh consumption, processing into pulp and juices, and significant export potential.
season: Flowering in winter, harvest in summer
cropDuration: 4-6 years to first fruiting, productive for decades

soil.type: Deep, well-drained alluvial to laterite soil
soil.drainage: Deep well-drained soil essential for root development

climate.rainfall: 750-2500 mm, dry period needed for flowering

waterRequirement: Moderate; irrigation critical during flowering and fruit development, avoid irrigation during dormancy

yield.profitability: High profitability with strong domestic and export markets

marketDemand: Very strong domestic demand and significant export market, especially for Alphonso, Kesar, and Dasheri varieties

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.purpose: Improves soil organic matter and root health

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses before flowering and after fruit set

majorDiseases.name: Anthracnose
majorDiseases.symptoms: Black sunken spots on fruits and leaves, twig blight
majorDiseases.management: Spray Carbendazim, prune affected twigs, post-harvest hot water treatment

majorDiseases.name: Powdery Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: White powdery growth on inflorescence and young leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Wettable sulphur during flowering, avoid excess humidity

majorDiseases.name: Malformation
majorDiseases.symptoms: Abnormal vegetative or floral growth resembling witches broom
majorDiseases.management: Prune affected panicles, spray NAA, avoid excess nitrogen

pests.name: Mango Hopper
pests.damage: Sucks sap from inflorescence causing flower drop
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid at panicle emergence and fruit set stage

pests.name: Fruit Fly
pests.damage: Larvae infest ripening fruits causing rotting
pests.control: Methyl eugenol traps, bagging fruits, field sanitation

pests.name: Mealybug
pests.damage: Sucks sap from tender shoots and inflorescence
pests.control: Banding trunk with sticky material, spray neem oil

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Dig pits of 1x1x1 m at spacing of 10x10 m for standard varieties; fill with topsoil, FYM and sand mixture, allowing settling before planting.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select grafted saplings of recommended variety from certified nursery, ensuring healthy rootstock and disease-free scion wood.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Plant grafted saplings during onset of monsoon, ensuring graft union remains above soil level to prevent collar rot.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide regular irrigation during initial establishment years; mature trees need irrigation during flowering and fruit development, with dry spell before flowering.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply organic manure and balanced NPK annually in tree basins after harvest; foliar micronutrient sprays improve flowering and fruit set.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Maintain weed-free basins through mulching and shallow cultivation; avoid deep digging near root zone of mature trees.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for hoppers during flowering and fruit flies during fruiting; adopt integrated management combining traps, sanitation and sprays.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest mature fruits at proper maturity stage (based on variety-specific indicators) using harvesting poles with attached bags to avoid bruising.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Handle harvested fruits carefully, treat with hot water for anthracnose control, grade by size and ripen uniformly before market dispatch.

advantages: Strong brand value for regional varieties
challenges: Susceptible to hopper and anthracnose damage
Sensitive to untimely rainfall during flowering
storageGuidelines: Store mature green mangoes at 13°C with 85-90% humidity for transport; ripen using controlled ethylene treatment for uniform color and quality.

name: Orange
overview: Orange, including mandarin varieties, is an important citrus fruit crop grown widely in central and northeastern India, valued for fresh consumption and juice processing with significant nutritional benefits.
season: Flowering in spring, harvest in winter
cropDuration: 3-4 years to first fruiting, productive for 20-25 years

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage critical, intolerant of waterlogging

waterRequirement: 900-1200 mm annually; critical irrigation at flowering and fruit development

yield.profitability: Good profitability with steady regional demand

marketDemand: Strong regional demand for fresh fruit and growing juice processing industry

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.purpose: Improves soil fertility and root health

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses during pre-flowering and post fruit-set

fertilizers.name: Potassium (MOP)
fertilizers.purpose: Improves fruit size and juice quality

majorDiseases.name: Citrus Canker
majorDiseases.symptoms: Raised corky lesions on leaves, twigs and fruit
majorDiseases.management: Spray Copper oxychloride, prune affected parts, avoid overhead irrigation

majorDiseases.name: Gummosis (Foot Rot)
majorDiseases.symptoms: Gum exudation from trunk bark with bark cracking
majorDiseases.management: Avoid waterlogging, apply Bordeaux paste, improve basin drainage

majorDiseases.name: Citrus Greening (Huanglongbing)
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellow mottling of leaves, lopsided small fruits
majorDiseases.management: Control psyllid vector, remove infected trees, use disease-free planting material

pests.name: Citrus Psylla
pests.damage: Transmits greening disease, damages new shoots
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid on new flush, prune affected shoots

pests.name: Fruit Sucking Moth
pests.damage: Punctures fruit causing rotting and premature drop
pests.control: Light traps, field sanitation, bagging of fruits

pests.name: Citrus Leaf Miner
pests.damage: Mines into young leaves causing distorted growth
pests.control: Spray neem oil on new flush growth

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Dig pits of 75x75x75 cm at spacing of 6x6 m; fill with topsoil, FYM and sand mixture, allowing settling before planting.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select healthy grafted or budded saplings from certified nursery, free from greening disease and canker symptoms.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Plant saplings during monsoon onset, ensuring bud union remains above soil level; provide stakes for initial support.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide regular irrigation during establishment and critical flowering/fruiting stages; avoid waterlogging in basins.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply organic manure and balanced NPK annually before flowering; supplement with micronutrients like zinc for healthy foliage.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Maintain weed-free basins through mulching; avoid mechanical damage to trunk and root zone during cultivation.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for psylla and canker regularly, especially on new flush growth; adopt integrated management with timely pruning and spraying.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest fruits when they attain full color and characteristic taste, typically November to January, by clipping with stalk intact.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Handle harvested fruits gently to avoid skin damage, grade by size, and store in cool, ventilated conditions for market dispatch.

challenges: Highly susceptible to citrus greening disease
Vulnerable to canker in humid regions
storageGuidelines: Store harvested fruits at 4-7°C with 85-90% humidity for extended shelf life; avoid storage near ethylene-producing fruits to prevent quality deterioration.

name: Ragi
overview: Ragi, or finger millet, is a hardy nutri-cereal crop traditionally grown in hilly and semi-arid regions of India, valued for its high calcium content, drought tolerance, and importance in millet-based nutrition programs.

soil.type: Red loamy to lateritic soil
soil.drainage: Tolerant of marginal soils with moderate drainage

waterRequirement: 400-600 mm; relatively low water requirement, suited to rainfed conditions

yield.profitability: Moderate profitability, rising with millet nutrition initiatives

marketDemand: Growing demand due to health consciousness and government millet promotion programs

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at sowing and 30 days after sowing

fertilizers.name: Potassium (MOP)
fertilizers.purpose: Improves grain filling and disease resistance

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.purpose: Improves soil fertility in marginal lands

majorDiseases.name: Blast Disease
majorDiseases.symptoms: Spindle-shaped lesions on leaves and neck region
majorDiseases.management: Spray Tricyclazole, avoid excess nitrogen, use resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Smut
majorDiseases.symptoms: Grain converted into black powdery mass
majorDiseases.management: Seed treatment with Carbendazim, field sanitation

majorDiseases.name: Mottled Leaf Disease
majorDiseases.symptoms: Mottling and streaking on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Use disease-free seed, remove infected plants

pests.name: Stem Borer
pests.damage: Bores into stem causing deadheart
pests.control: Apply Carbofuran granules, timely sowing

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Sucks sap from earhead causing chaffy grains
pests.control: Spray Malathion or neem-based insecticide

pests.name: Earhead Bug
pests.damage: Sucks grain sap reducing grain quality
pests.control: Spray recommended insecticide at ear emergence

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field with onset of pre-monsoon rains; prepare fine seedbed suitable for fine-seeded ragi through repeated harrowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select improved or local high-yielding variety seeds with good germination; treat seeds with fungicide before sowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds either by broadcasting or transplanting nursery-raised seedlings at spacing of 22.5x10 cm for better yield.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Largely rainfed; provide supplemental irrigation during prolonged dry spells, especially at flowering and grain filling stages.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply basal fertilizers at sowing/transplanting, top-dress nitrogen at active tillering stage approximately 30 days later.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct first weeding at 20-25 days after sowing/transplanting; ensure field remains weed-free during critical growth period.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for blast disease and stem borer; remove and destroy infected plants, use recommended pesticides as needed.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when earheads turn brown and grains harden, typically 100-120 days after sowing, by cutting earheads or whole plants.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested earheads in sun, thresh to separate grain, clean and store at safe moisture levels in airtight containers.

advantages: Suitable for marginal and hilly lands
Growing market due to health trends
challenges: Lower yield potential than major cereals
Susceptible to blast in humid conditions
storageGuidelines: Dry grains to 12% moisture before storage; store in airtight containers as ragi has good inherent storage life with minimal pest infestation.

name: Soybean
overview: Soybean is a major oilseed and protein-rich legume crop grown extensively in central India, serving as a key raw material for edible oil and soymeal production for the animal feed industry.

soil.type: Well-drained black soil to loamy soil
soil.drainage: Moderate drainage, sensitive to waterlogging

waterRequirement: 450-700 mm; sensitive to both drought and waterlogging at flowering

yield.profitability: Good profitability supported by oil and feed industry demand

marketDemand: Strong demand from edible oil extraction industry and soymeal for poultry/cattle feed

majorDiseases.name: Yellow Mosaic Virus
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellow mosaic patches on leaves, stunted growth
majorDiseases.management: Control whitefly vector, use resistant varieties, remove infected plants

majorDiseases.name: Rust
majorDiseases.symptoms: Reddish-brown pustules on leaf undersurface
majorDiseases.management: Spray Hexaconazole, timely sowing, resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Charcoal Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Greying and shredding of stem tissue at base
majorDiseases.management: Avoid moisture stress, balanced fertilization, crop rotation

pests.name: Stem Fly
pests.damage: Larvae bore into stem causing wilting of plants
pests.control: Seed treatment with Thiamethoxam, timely sowing

pests.name: Girdle Beetle
pests.damage: Girdles stem and petiole causing plant lodging
pests.control: Spray Chlorpyrifos, remove and destroy affected plants

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.damage: Transmits yellow mosaic virus
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid, use yellow sticky traps

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field with pre-monsoon showers; harrow to prepare fine, weed-free seedbed with good moisture conservation.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified high-yielding variety seeds with good germination percentage; treat seeds with Rhizobium and fungicide.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 3-4 cm depth with row spacing of 30-45 cm and plant spacing of 5-10 cm using seed drill for uniform stand.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Largely rainfed; ensure good drainage to avoid waterlogging, provide supplemental irrigation during dry spells at flowering.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and starter nitrogen as basal dose; rely on nodulation for subsequent nitrogen needs through Rhizobium inoculation.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct first weeding at 20-25 days after sowing; pre-emergence herbicide like Pendimethalin can be used for early weed control.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for stem fly, girdle beetle and yellow mosaic virus; adopt integrated pest management with timely insecticide application.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when pods turn brown and leaves begin to shed, typically 90-110 days after sowing, using sickles or combine harvesters.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested plants, thresh to separate seeds, clean and dry to 10-12% moisture before storage in moisture-proof bags.

challenges: Price dependent on international oilseed markets
Limited irrigation infrastructure in growing regions
storageGuidelines: Dry seeds to 10-12% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry conditions in moisture-proof bags to maintain oil quality and prevent rancidity.

name: Potato
overview: Potato is one of the most important tuber crops in India, grown extensively in the Rabi season in the Indo-Gangetic plains, valued for its versatility as a staple food, processing raw material, and export commodity.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam soil rich in organic matter
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential for tuber development

waterRequirement: 500-700 mm; light frequent irrigation through furrows or sprinklers

yield.profitability: Good profitability with strong processing and export demand

marketDemand: High and consistent demand for fresh consumption, processing (chips, fries), and seed potato trade

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.purpose: Improves soil structure for tuber development

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at planting and 30 days after

fertilizers.name: Potassium (MOP)
fertilizers.purpose: Improves tuber quality and starch content

majorDiseases.name: Late Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves turning brown with white mold underneath
majorDiseases.management: Spray Metalaxyl + Mancozeb, use resistant varieties, avoid overhead irrigation

majorDiseases.name: Early Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Concentric brown spots on older leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, balanced fertilization, crop rotation

majorDiseases.name: Common Scab
majorDiseases.symptoms: Rough corky lesions on tuber surface
majorDiseases.management: Maintain soil pH below 5.5, avoid liming, use disease-free seed tubers

pests.name: Potato Tuber Moth
pests.damage: Larvae bore into tubers causing tunneling and rotting
pests.control: Proper earthing up, store in cool dark conditions, use pheromone traps

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Transmits viral diseases and causes leaf curling
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid, use virus-free seed tubers

pests.name: Cutworm
pests.damage: Cuts young plants at soil level
pests.control: Apply Chlorpyrifos dust around plant base

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field deeply followed by harrowing to create fine, loose tilth essential for tuber expansion; form ridges and furrows.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified disease-free seed tubers of recommended variety, treated with fungicide; use tubers of 30-40g weight for planting.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Plant seed tubers at 5-7 cm depth with spacing of 60x20 cm in furrows, ensuring sprouted eyes face upward.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Irrigate immediately after planting and maintain regular light irrigation; critical stages include tuber initiation and bulking.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply fertilizers in split doses with full phosphorus and potassium at planting; top-dress nitrogen at 30 days followed by earthing up.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct earthing up at 30 and 45 days after planting to cover developing tubers and suppress weeds simultaneously.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for late blight especially during humid winter conditions; spray preventive fungicides and control aphid vectors regularly.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when foliage yellows and dries naturally, typically 90-120 days after planting, using diggers to avoid tuber damage.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Cure harvested tubers in shade for a few days, grade by size, and store in cool, dark, well-ventilated cold storage facilities.

advantages: Good storage potential with cold storage
challenges: Dependent on quality seed tuber availability
Price volatility due to overproduction years
storageGuidelines: Cure tubers before storage; store in cold storage at 2-4°C with 90-95% humidity for long-term storage, or in well-ventilated structures for short-term use.

name: Mustard
overview: Mustard is a major Rabi oilseed crop of India, primarily grown for its oil-rich seeds used in cooking oil and condiment production, with strong adaptability to cooler climates of northern India.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential, intolerant of waterlogging

waterRequirement: 250-400 mm; critical irrigation at flowering and pod formation

yield.profitability: Good profitability with steady edible oil demand

marketDemand: Strong demand from edible oil industry and condiment manufacturing

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at sowing and first irrigation

fertilizers.name: Sulphur
fertilizers.purpose: Improves oil content and quality significantly

fertilizers.name: Potassium (MOP)
fertilizers.purpose: Improves disease resistance and seed quality

majorDiseases.name: Alternaria Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Dark brown circular spots on leaves and pods
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, use resistant varieties, timely sowing

majorDiseases.name: White Rust
majorDiseases.symptoms: White pustules on leaf undersurface, distorted inflorescence
majorDiseases.management: Spray Metalaxyl + Mancozeb, resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Powdery Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves and stems
majorDiseases.management: Spray Wettable sulphur, avoid dense planting

pests.name: Mustard Aphid
pests.damage: Sucks sap causing curling of leaves and yield loss
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid or Dimethoate at early infestation

pests.name: Painted Bug
pests.damage: Sucks sap from seedlings causing wilting
pests.control: Spray Malathion, deep summer ploughing to destroy eggs

pests.name: Sawfly
pests.damage: Larvae feed on leaves causing defoliation
pests.control: Spray Quinalphos at early larval stage

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field after Kharif harvest, followed by harrowing to prepare fine seedbed with adequate residual moisture.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified high-yielding variety seeds with good oil content; treat seeds with fungicide and insecticide before sowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 2-3 cm depth with row spacing of 30-45 cm using seed drill, ensuring optimal plant population through thinning.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide first irrigation at 25-30 days after sowing, with subsequent irrigation at flowering and pod formation stages.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and sulphur as basal dose for enhanced oil content; top-dress nitrogen at first irrigation for vegetative growth.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct thinning and first weeding at 20-25 days after sowing to maintain optimal plant spacing and reduce weed competition.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for aphid infestation regularly, especially during cool weather; adopt timely spraying to prevent population buildup.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when pods turn yellowish-brown and seeds rattle inside, typically 110-140 days after sowing, by cutting plants at base.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested plants in sun, thresh to separate seeds, clean and store at 8-9% moisture in moisture-proof containers.

advantages: Good for rainfed and irrigated rabi systems
High oil content with industrial value
challenges: Susceptible to aphid infestation in cool weather
Price linked to edible oil import policy
Requires timely sowing for optimal yield
storageGuidelines: Dry seeds to 8-9% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry conditions in airtight containers to preserve oil quality and prevent rancidity.

name: Sesame
overview: Sesame, known as til, is an ancient oilseed crop grown across India for its high-quality edible oil and use in confectionery and traditional foods, valued for drought tolerance and short duration.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential, intolerant of waterlogging

waterRequirement: 350-500 mm; sensitive to waterlogging especially at germination

yield.profitability: Good profitability due to high oil value and export demand

marketDemand: Strong export demand for sesame seeds and oil, particularly to Asian and Middle Eastern markets

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at sowing and 30 days after sowing

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.purpose: Improves soil fertility and moisture retention

majorDiseases.name: Phyllody Disease
majorDiseases.symptoms: Floral parts transformed into leafy structures
majorDiseases.management: Control leafhopper vector, remove infected plants, use resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Charcoal Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Greying and shredding of stem at base
majorDiseases.management: Avoid moisture stress, crop rotation, balanced fertilization

majorDiseases.name: Leaf Spot (Cercospora)
majorDiseases.symptoms: Small circular brown spots on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, field sanitation

pests.name: Gall Fly
pests.damage: Forms galls on stem affecting plant growth
pests.control: Remove and destroy affected stems, spray recommended insecticide

pests.name: Leafhopper
pests.damage: Transmits phyllody disease and sucks sap
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid, remove weed hosts

pests.name: Capsule Borer
pests.damage: Bores into capsules causing seed loss
pests.control: Spray Quinalphos at capsule formation stage

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field with onset of season; prepare fine, well-leveled seedbed since sesame seeds are small and require shallow sowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified high-yielding variety seeds with good oil content; treat seeds with fungicide before sowing for disease prevention.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 1-2 cm shallow depth with row spacing of 30-45 cm, either by broadcasting or line sowing using seed drill.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Ensure good drainage as sesame is highly sensitive to waterlogging; provide light irrigation during dry spells if available.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply basal fertilizers at sowing with phosphorus and sulphur for oil content; top-dress nitrogen at 30 days after sowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct thinning and first weeding at 15-20 days after sowing; maintain weed-free field during initial growth period.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for leafhopper-transmitted phyllody disease and capsule borer; remove infected plants promptly to prevent spread.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when lower leaves turn yellow and capsules begin to crack, typically 80-100 days after sowing, by cutting plants at base.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Stack harvested plants for sun drying, thresh by beating to release seeds, clean and dry to 6-7% moisture before storage.

advantages: High oil content with premium pricing
storageGuidelines: Dry seeds to 6-7% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry conditions in airtight containers to preserve oil quality and prevent rancidity.

name: Jute
overview: Jute is the golden fiber crop of India, predominantly cultivated in the eastern Gangetic delta region, valued for its strong natural fiber used in sacking, packaging, and increasingly in eco-friendly textile products.

soil.type: Alluvial loam to clay loam soil
soil.drainage: Moderate drainage, tolerates temporary waterlogging

waterRequirement: 1000-1500 mm during growth, retting requires standing water

yield.profitability: Moderate profitability, supported by jute industry demand

marketDemand: Steady demand from jute mills for sacking, carpet backing, and growing demand for eco-friendly packaging

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at sowing and 30 days after sowing

majorDiseases.name: Stem Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Black lesions on stem leading to breakage
majorDiseases.management: Use resistant varieties, avoid waterlogging at early stage, seed treatment

majorDiseases.name: Anthracnose
majorDiseases.symptoms: Dark sunken lesions on stem and leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Carbendazim, crop rotation

majorDiseases.name: Leaf Mosaic
majorDiseases.symptoms: Mottled yellow-green pattern on leaves
