majorDiseases.management: Control insect vectors, use disease-free seed

pests.name: Jute Semilooper
pests.damage: Defoliates plants reducing fiber yield
pests.control: Spray Quinalphos, remove and destroy affected leaves

pests.name: Stem Weevil
pests.damage: Bores into stem causing breakage and poor fiber quality
pests.control: Field sanitation, spray Chlorpyrifos at early infestation

pests.name: Hairy Caterpillar
pests.damage: Feeds on leaves causing defoliation
pests.control: Hand picking of egg masses, spray recommended insecticide

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field thoroughly with pre-monsoon rains; prepare fine, well-leveled seedbed for uniform germination of small seeds.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified high-fiber-yielding variety seeds; treat seeds with fungicide before sowing for disease-free establishment.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds by broadcasting or line sowing at shallow depth with row spacing of 25-30 cm, ensuring dense uniform plant stand.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide irrigation if pre-monsoon rains are insufficient; standing water required during retting process after harvest.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply basal fertilizers at sowing followed by top-dressing of nitrogen at 30 days after sowing for vigorous fiber development.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct thinning at 15-20 days after sowing to maintain optimal plant density; weeding done manually in early growth stage.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for semilooper and stem weevil infestation; adopt timely spraying and field sanitation to minimize fiber quality loss.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest at flowering stage when fiber quality is optimal, typically 100-120 days after sowing, by cutting plants close to ground.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Retting of harvested stalks in clean water for 10-15 days, followed by fiber extraction, washing, and sun drying before baling.

advantages: Strong industrial demand for sacking and packaging
By-product use as fuel and fodder
challenges: Labor-intensive retting and fiber extraction process
Quality dependent on retting water cleanliness
Limited price realization compared to synthetic fibers
Susceptible to stem rot in poor drainage
intercroppingOptions: Not commonly intercropped; rotated with rice or vegetables
storageGuidelines: Dry extracted fiber thoroughly before baling; store in well-ventilated, dry godowns away from moisture to prevent fungal degradation of fiber quality.

name: Wheat
overview: Wheat is the second most important staple food crop of India after rice, predominantly grown in the Rabi season across the Indo-Gangetic plains, forming a critical component of national food security.

soil.type: Well-drained loamy to clay loam soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage with adequate moisture retention

climate.temperature: 10-25°C optimum, cool during growth, warm at maturity

waterRequirement: 450-650 mm; 4-6 irrigations at critical growth stages

yield.profitability: Good profitability supported by government MSP procurement

marketDemand: Assured demand through government procurement and strong domestic flour milling industry

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at sowing, first irrigation (CRI stage), and later tillering

fertilizers.name: Zinc Sulphate
fertilizers.purpose: Corrects zinc deficiency common in wheat belts

majorDiseases.name: Rust (Yellow, Brown, Black)
majorDiseases.symptoms: Orange to brown pustules on leaves and stems
majorDiseases.management: Spray Propiconazole, use resistant varieties, timely sowing

majorDiseases.name: Karnal Bunt
majorDiseases.symptoms: Partial bunting of grains with fishy odor
majorDiseases.management: Use disease-free seed, seed treatment with fungicide, resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Powdery Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves and stems
majorDiseases.management: Spray Wettable sulphur, avoid excess nitrogen

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Sucks sap from leaves and earheads causing reduced grain weight
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid, conserve natural predators like ladybird beetles

pests.name: Termites
pests.damage: Damages roots and stem base causing wilting
pests.control: Soil treatment with Chlorpyrifos before sowing

pests.name: Armyworm
pests.damage: Feeds on leaves and earheads in large numbers
pests.control: Spray Quinalphos at early larval stage

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field 2-3 times after Kharif harvest, followed by planking to conserve moisture and create fine, firm seedbed.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified high-yielding variety seeds suited to sowing time and irrigation availability; treat seeds with fungicide.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 5-6 cm depth with row spacing of 18-22 cm using seed-cum-fertilizer drill for uniform placement and early vigor.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide first irrigation at crown root initiation stage (20-25 days), followed by irrigations at tillering, flowering, and grain filling.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and potassium as full basal dose; top-dress nitrogen in split doses at CRI and later tillering stages.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct weeding or apply post-emergence herbicide like 2,4-D at 30-35 days after sowing to control broadleaf weeds.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for rust diseases and aphid infestation, especially during humid weather; spray fungicides and insecticides as needed.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when crop turns golden yellow and grains harden, typically 120-150 days after sowing, using sickles or combine harvesters.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Thresh promptly, clean grains, dry to 12% moisture, and store in moisture-proof bags or silos to prevent insect infestation.

advantages: High calorie staple with consistent demand
storageGuidelines: Dry grains to 12% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry, well-ventilated godowns with regular fumigation to prevent weevil and rodent damage.

name: Chickpea
overview: Chickpea, known as chana, is the most important Rabi pulse crop of India, valued for its high protein content and versatility in culinary use, while also enriching soil fertility through nitrogen fixation.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam to clay loam soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential, intolerant of waterlogging

climate.temperature: 15-25°C optimum, cool growth and warm maturity

waterRequirement: 250-400 mm; often grown on residual soil moisture with 1-2 irrigations

yield.profitability: Good profitability supported by MSP and strong domestic demand

marketDemand: High and stable demand as a key protein source in Indian diet, supported by government procurement

majorDiseases.name: Fusarium Wilt
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of plants, vascular browning
majorDiseases.management: Use resistant varieties, crop rotation, seed treatment with Trichoderma

majorDiseases.name: Ascochyta Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Dark concentric lesions on leaves, stem and pods
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, use disease-free seed, resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Botrytis Grey Mold
majorDiseases.symptoms: Grey fungal growth on flowers and pods in humid conditions
majorDiseases.management: Spray Carbendazim, avoid dense planting

pests.name: Pod Borer (Helicoverpa)
pests.damage: Bores into developing pods causing seed loss
pests.control: Use pheromone traps, spray Spinosad or NPV at early larval stage

pests.name: Cutworm
pests.damage: Cuts seedlings at soil level during night
pests.control: Apply Chlorpyrifos dust around plant base

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Sucks sap causing stunted growth
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid at early infestation

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field after Kharif harvest, harrow to prepare fine seedbed that conserves residual moisture for rainfed cultivation.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified disease-resistant variety seeds, particularly wilt-resistant types; treat seeds with Rhizobium and fungicide.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 8-10 cm depth with row spacing of 30 cm using seed drill, ensuring good seed-to-soil contact for germination.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Largely grown on residual moisture; provide one or two light irrigations at branching and pod development stages if available.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and starter nitrogen as basal dose; rely on Rhizobium-mediated nitrogen fixation for crop nitrogen needs.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct one or two weedings at 25-30 and 45 days after sowing; pre-emergence herbicide like Pendimethalin can be used.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for pod borer infestation closely as it causes major yield loss; install pheromone traps and spray at threshold levels.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when plants turn yellowish-brown and pods rattle with dry seeds, typically 90-120 days after sowing, by uprooting or cutting.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested plants in field, thresh to separate seeds, clean and store at 10-12% moisture in pest-proof containers.

advantages: High protein content valuable in vegetarian diets
Suitable for rainfed residual moisture cultivation
Strong domestic market with MSP support
challenges: Highly susceptible to pod borer damage
Vulnerable to wilt in continuous cropping
Yield variability with erratic winter rains
storageGuidelines: Dry seeds to 10-12% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry conditions in pest-proof bags or bins to prevent bruchid beetle infestation.

name: Lentil
overview: Lentil, known as masoor, is an important Rabi pulse crop grown widely in the Indo-Gangetic plains, valued for its high protein content, short duration, and ability to improve soil fertility through nitrogen fixation.

soil.type: Well-drained loamy soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential, intolerant of waterlogging

waterRequirement: 200-350 mm; often grown on residual moisture with minimal irrigation

yield.profitability: Moderate to good profitability with steady domestic demand

marketDemand: Consistent demand as a key pulse in Indian diet, with growing import substitution focus

fertilizers.name: Sulphur
fertilizers.purpose: Improves seed quality and oil content

majorDiseases.name: Rust
majorDiseases.symptoms: Reddish-brown pustules on leaves and stems
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, use resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Wilt
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellowing and drooping of plants, root rot
majorDiseases.management: Crop rotation, seed treatment with Trichoderma, resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Powdery Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Wettable sulphur, avoid dense planting

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Sucks sap causing stunted growth and reduced pod set
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid at early infestation

pests.name: Pod Borer
pests.damage: Bores into pods causing seed damage
pests.control: Spray Spinosad, use pheromone traps

pests.name: Cutworm
pests.damage: Cuts seedlings at soil level
pests.control: Soil application of Chlorpyrifos before sowing

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field after Kharif harvest, harrow to prepare fine seedbed that retains residual soil moisture for rainfed growth.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified disease-resistant variety seeds with good germination percentage; treat seeds with Rhizobium and fungicide.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 5-6 cm depth with row spacing of 25-30 cm using seed drill, ensuring uniform plant stand for optimal yield.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Largely grown on residual moisture; provide one light irrigation at flowering/pod formation stage if water is available.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and starter nitrogen as basal dose; nodulation through Rhizobium provides subsequent nitrogen requirement.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct one weeding at 30-35 days after sowing; pre-emergence herbicide application can reduce manual weeding labor.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for aphid and pod borer infestation during flowering and pod development; spray recommended insecticides as needed.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when pods turn brown and plants dry, typically 100-120 days after sowing, by uprooting plants or cutting at base.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested plants in field, thresh to separate seeds, clean and store at 10-12% moisture in pest-proof containers.

advantages: Short duration suited to rabi cropping window
challenges: Susceptible to rust in humid conditions
storageGuidelines: Dry seeds to 10-12% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry conditions in pest-proof containers to prevent bruchid beetle infestation.

name: Urad
overview: Urad, or black gram, is a short-duration pulse crop grown across Kharif and Rabi seasons in India, valued for its high protein content and traditional use in dal, idli, and dosa preparations.

soil.type: Well-drained loamy soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential, intolerant of waterlogging

waterRequirement: 300-400 mm; sensitive to both drought and excess moisture

yield.profitability: Good profitability supported by strong domestic pulse demand

marketDemand: High and steady demand as a key ingredient in South Indian cuisine and dal preparations

majorDiseases.name: Yellow Mosaic Virus
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellow mosaic patches on leaves, stunted growth
majorDiseases.management: Control whitefly vector, use resistant varieties, remove infected plants

majorDiseases.name: Powdery Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Wettable sulphur, avoid dense planting

majorDiseases.name: Cercospora Leaf Spot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Circular brown spots with yellow halo on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, field sanitation

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.damage: Transmits yellow mosaic virus and sucks sap
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid, use yellow sticky traps

pests.name: Pod Borer
pests.damage: Bores into pods causing seed loss
pests.control: Spray Spinosad, use pheromone traps

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Sucks sap causing stunted growth
pests.control: Spray neem oil or Dimethoate

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field lightly with onset of monsoon; harrow to prepare fine, weed-free seedbed for quick germination.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified high-yielding variety seeds resistant to yellow mosaic virus; treat seeds with Rhizobium and fungicide.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 3-4 cm depth with row spacing of 30 cm using seed drill or broadcasting followed by light covering.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Largely rainfed; ensure good drainage to avoid waterlogging, provide light irrigation during dry spells if available.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and starter nitrogen as basal dose; rely on Rhizobium nodulation for subsequent nitrogen needs.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct one weeding at 20-25 days after sowing; maintain weed-free field during critical early growth period.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for whitefly-transmitted yellow mosaic virus closely; remove infected plants and spray insecticides at early infestation.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when pods turn black and dry, typically 70-90 days after sowing, by uprooting plants or cutting at base.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested plants in field, thresh to separate seeds, clean and store at 10-12% moisture in pest-proof containers.

advantages: Short duration fits multiple cropping systems
challenges: Highly susceptible to yellow mosaic virus
Pod shattering at maturity causes losses
storageGuidelines: Dry seeds to 10-12% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry conditions in pest-proof containers to prevent bruchid beetle infestation.

name: Sunflower
overview: Sunflower is an important oilseed crop grown across multiple seasons in India, valued for its high-quality edible oil and adaptability to diverse agro-climatic conditions including dryland farming.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam to black soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential

waterRequirement: 400-600 mm; critical irrigation at flowering and seed filling stages

yield.profitability: Good profitability driven by edible oil demand

marketDemand: Strong demand from edible oil industry, supplementing domestic oilseed production

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at sowing and 30 days after sowing

fertilizers.name: Boron
fertilizers.purpose: Improves seed setting and prevents hollow heads
fertilizers.applicationStage: Foliar spray at flower bud stage

majorDiseases.name: Alternaria Leaf Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Dark brown circular spots on leaves coalescing into blight
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, crop rotation, resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Head Rot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Rotting of flower head in humid conditions
majorDiseases.management: Avoid excess moisture at flowering, spray Carbendazim

majorDiseases.name: Downy Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: Chlorotic patches on leaves with downy growth underneath
majorDiseases.management: Seed treatment with Metalaxyl, resistant varieties

pests.name: Head Borer
pests.damage: Bores into developing head causing seed damage
pests.control: Spray Spinosad at flowering stage, use pheromone traps

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Sucks sap causing stunted growth
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid at early infestation

pests.name: Capitulum Borer
pests.damage: Feeds on developing seeds within head
pests.control: Spray recommended insecticide at bud stage

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field deeply followed by harrowing to prepare fine, weed-free seedbed suitable for direct sowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified hybrid variety seeds with high oil content; treat seeds with fungicide before sowing for disease prevention.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 4-5 cm depth with row spacing of 45-60 cm and plant spacing of 20-25 cm using seed drill or dibbling.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide irrigation at critical stages including bud formation, flowering, and seed filling; avoid water stress during these phases.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and potassium as basal dose; top-dress nitrogen at 30 days after sowing along with foliar boron at flowering.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct first weeding at 20-25 days after sowing; maintain weed-free field particularly during early vegetative stage.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for head borer and Alternaria blight regularly; adopt timely spraying especially during flowering and head formation.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when back of head turns yellow-brown and seeds harden, typically 90-110 days after sowing, by cutting heads or whole plants.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested heads in sun, thresh to separate seeds, clean and dry to 8-9% moisture before storage in moisture-proof bags.

advantages: Suitable for dryland and irrigated cultivation
challenges: Susceptible to bird damage at maturity
Vulnerable to head rot in humid conditions
Requires manual pollination support in some cases
Price dependent on edible oil import policy
storageGuidelines: Dry seeds to 8-9% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry conditions in moisture-proof containers to prevent rancidity and pest infestation.

name: Barley
overview: Barley is a hardy Rabi cereal crop grown in semi-arid and saline-affected regions of India, valued for malting, animal feed, and traditional food use, with notable tolerance to soil salinity and drought.

soil.type: Sandy loam to saline-alkaline soil
soil.drainage: Tolerant of moderate salinity and marginal drainage

waterRequirement: 300-450 mm; lower water requirement than wheat, 2-3 irrigations sufficient

yield.profitability: Moderate profitability with growing malting industry demand

marketDemand: Steady demand from malting and brewing industry alongside traditional food and feed use

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses at sowing and first irrigation

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.purpose: Improves soil structure in marginal lands

majorDiseases.name: Yellow Rust
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellow striped pustules on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Propiconazole, use resistant varieties

majorDiseases.name: Loose Smut
majorDiseases.symptoms: Grains replaced by black powdery mass at earhead
majorDiseases.management: Seed treatment with Carboxin, use disease-free seed

majorDiseases.name: Covered Smut
majorDiseases.symptoms: Grains converted to smut sori covered by membrane
majorDiseases.management: Seed treatment with systemic fungicide

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Sucks sap from leaves and earheads
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid, conserve natural predators

pests.name: Termites
pests.damage: Damages roots and stem base
pests.control: Soil treatment with Chlorpyrifos before sowing

pests.name: Armyworm
pests.damage: Feeds on leaves and earheads
pests.control: Spray Quinalphos at early infestation

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field after Kharif harvest, followed by planking to conserve moisture; suitable even on marginal saline-affected lands.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified variety seeds suited to malting or feed purpose with good germination; treat seeds with fungicide before sowing.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 5-6 cm depth with row spacing of 20-22 cm using seed drill for uniform placement and early establishment.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Provide first irrigation at crown root initiation stage, with subsequent irrigation at tillering and grain filling if needed.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and potassium as basal dose; top-dress nitrogen in split doses at sowing and first irrigation stage.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct weeding at 30-35 days after sowing or apply post-emergence herbicide to control broadleaf and grassy weeds.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for rust and smut diseases regularly; use disease-free certified seed as the primary preventive measure.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when crop turns golden yellow and grains harden, typically 110-130 days after sowing, using sickles or combine harvesters.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Thresh promptly, clean grains, dry to 12% moisture, and store in moisture-proof bags for malting or feed industry supply.

advantages: Tolerant of saline and marginal soils
challenges: Limited large-scale processing infrastructure outside malting
Susceptible to rust in humid conditions
storageGuidelines: Dry grains to 12% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry, well-ventilated conditions, particularly maintaining low moisture for malting quality preservation.

name: Pigeonpea
overview: Pigeonpea, known as arhar or tur, is a major long-duration pulse crop of India grown extensively in rainfed conditions, valued for its high protein content and deep root system that improves soil structure.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam to black soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential, deep-rooted crop tolerant of moisture stress

waterRequirement: 500-700 mm; deep root system provides drought tolerance, minimal irrigation needed

yield.profitability: Good profitability with strong domestic pulse demand

marketDemand: High and stable demand as a key pulse (dal) crop with growing import substitution efforts

majorDiseases.name: Fusarium Wilt
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of plants, vascular discoloration
majorDiseases.management: Use resistant varieties, crop rotation, seed treatment with Trichoderma

majorDiseases.name: Sterility Mosaic Disease
majorDiseases.symptoms: Excessive vegetative growth with reduced flowering
majorDiseases.management: Control mite vector, use resistant varieties, remove infected plants

majorDiseases.name: Phytophthora Blight
majorDiseases.symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on stem and leaves leading to plant death
majorDiseases.management: Improve drainage, spray Metalaxyl, resistant varieties

pests.name: Pod Borer (Helicoverpa)
pests.damage: Bores into pods causing major seed loss
pests.control: Use pheromone traps, spray NPV or Spinosad at early larval stage

pests.name: Pod Fly
pests.damage: Larvae feed inside pods damaging developing seeds
pests.control: Spray Quinalphos at pod formation stage

pests.name: Blister Beetle
pests.damage: Feeds on flowers reducing pod set
pests.control: Hand collection and destruction, spray recommended insecticide

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field with pre-monsoon showers, harrow to prepare seedbed; deep root system benefits from loosened subsoil.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified disease-resistant variety seeds suited to duration requirements; treat seeds with Rhizobium and fungicide.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 4-5 cm depth with row spacing of 60-90 cm and plant spacing of 20-30 cm using seed drill or dibbling.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Largely rainfed due to deep root system providing drought tolerance; supplemental irrigation beneficial during pod development.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and starter nitrogen as basal dose; rely on Rhizobium nodulation for subsequent nitrogen requirements.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct weeding at 20-25 and 45 days after sowing; earthing up beneficial for lodging resistance in tall varieties.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor closely for pod borer infestation which causes major yield loss; install pheromone traps and spray at threshold levels.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when pods turn brown and dry, typically 150-180 days after sowing, by cutting plants at base or picking pods in stages.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested plants/pods in sun, thresh to separate seeds, clean and store at 10-12% moisture in pest-proof containers.

advantages: Drought tolerant with low water needs
challenges: Long crop duration ties up land
Highly susceptible to pod borer damage
Vulnerable to wilt in continuous cropping
storageGuidelines: Dry seeds to 10-12% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry conditions in pest-proof bags to prevent bruchid beetle and weevil infestation.

name: Grapes
overview: Grapes are a high-value horticultural crop grown predominantly in Maharashtra and Karnataka, valued for fresh table consumption, raisin production, and a growing domestic wine industry, requiring intensive management and trellising.
season: Pruning in October/April, harvest in winter/summer
cropDuration: 2-3 years to first commercial harvest, productive for 15-20 years

soil.type: Well-drained deep loamy to sandy loam soil
soil.drainage: Excellent drainage critical, intolerant of waterlogging

climate.humidity: Low humidity favorable for fruit quality

waterRequirement: 900-1100 mm annually; drip irrigation essential for water-use efficiency and quality

yield.profitability: High profitability with strong domestic and export markets

marketDemand: Strong domestic table grape demand and significant export market, particularly for seedless varieties

fertilizers.name: Farmyard Manure
fertilizers.purpose: Improves soil organic matter and structure

fertilizers.name: Nitrogen (Urea)
fertilizers.applicationStage: Split doses after pruning and before flowering

fertilizers.name: Potassium (MOP)
fertilizers.purpose: Improves berry size, color and sugar content

majorDiseases.name: Downy Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellow patches on upper leaf surface with white downy growth underneath
majorDiseases.management: Spray Metalaxyl + Mancozeb, ensure canopy aeration

majorDiseases.name: Powdery Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, shoots and berries
majorDiseases.management: Spray Wettable sulphur, avoid excess nitrogen

majorDiseases.name: Anthracnose
majorDiseases.symptoms: Dark sunken spots on shoots, leaves and berries
majorDiseases.management: Spray Carbendazim, prune affected parts

pests.name: Thrips
pests.damage: Causes scarring and silvering of berry surface
pests.control: Spray Fipronil, use blue sticky traps

pests.name: Mealybug
pests.damage: Sucks sap from shoots and bunches, promotes sooty mold
pests.control: Spray Chlorpyrifos, release natural predators

pests.name: Flea Beetle
pests.damage: Feeds on buds and young leaves after pruning
pests.control: Spray Quinalphos immediately after pruning

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Dig pits of 60x60x60 cm at spacing of 3x2 m; install trellis system (bower or telephone) before planting for vine support.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select disease-free rooted cuttings or grafted vines of high-yielding table or wine variety from certified nursery sources.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Plant rooted cuttings during favorable winter months, training young vines onto trellis wires as they establish and grow.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Install drip irrigation for precise water management; irrigation scheduling closely tied to pruning and fruiting cycles.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply organic manure and balanced NPK after each pruning cycle; foliar micronutrient sprays improve berry quality and color.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Maintain weed-free vine rows through mulching and shallow cultivation; canopy management critical for light penetration.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct two prunings annually (foundation and fruit pruning); monitor closely for downy mildew and thrips throughout season.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest bunches at optimum sugar content (measured by Brix), typically 90-110 days after fruit pruning, using sharp secateurs.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Handle harvested bunches carefully to prevent berry shatter, pre-cool immediately, grade and pack for fresh market or export.

advantages: High value crop with strong export potential
Multiple harvests possible with double pruning
Value addition through raisins and wine
challenges: Susceptible to downy and powdery mildew
Sensitive to unseasonal rainfall during harvest
intercroppingOptions: Not commonly intercropped due to intensive trellising system
storageGuidelines: Pre-cool harvested grapes immediately to 0-2°C with 90-95% humidity; use sulphur dioxide pads in packaging for extended shelf life during export transport.

name: Moong
overview: Moong, or green gram, is a short-duration pulse crop widely grown across Kharif, summer, and Rabi seasons in India, valued for quick returns, soil-enriching nitrogen fixation, and high nutritional content.

soil.type: Well-drained sandy loam to loamy soil
soil.drainage: Good drainage essential, intolerant of waterlogging

waterRequirement: 250-350 mm; sensitive to waterlogging, drought-tolerant once established

yield.profitability: Good profitability due to short duration enabling multiple cropping

marketDemand: High and steady demand as a quick-cooking pulse and popular ingredient in sprouts and traditional dishes

majorDiseases.name: Yellow Mosaic Virus
majorDiseases.symptoms: Yellow mosaic patches on leaves, stunted growth
majorDiseases.management: Control whitefly vector, use resistant varieties, remove infected plants

majorDiseases.name: Cercospora Leaf Spot
majorDiseases.symptoms: Circular brown spots with yellow halo on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Mancozeb, field sanitation

majorDiseases.name: Powdery Mildew
majorDiseases.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves
majorDiseases.management: Spray Wettable sulphur, avoid dense planting

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.damage: Transmits yellow mosaic virus and sucks sap
pests.control: Spray Imidacloprid, use yellow sticky traps

pests.name: Pod Borer
pests.damage: Bores into pods causing seed loss
pests.control: Spray Spinosad, use pheromone traps

pests.name: Aphids
pests.damage: Sucks sap causing stunted growth
pests.control: Spray neem oil or Dimethoate

cultivationProcess.title: Land Preparation
cultivationProcess.description: Plough field lightly with onset of season; harrow to prepare fine, weed-free seedbed for quick germination of small seeds.

cultivationProcess.title: Seed Selection
cultivationProcess.description: Select certified high-yielding variety seeds resistant to yellow mosaic virus; treat seeds with Rhizobium and fungicide.

cultivationProcess.title: Sowing or Planting
cultivationProcess.description: Sow seeds at 3-4 cm depth with row spacing of 30 cm using seed drill or broadcasting followed by light covering.

cultivationProcess.title: Irrigation Management
cultivationProcess.description: Largely rainfed or supplemental irrigation; ensure good drainage to avoid waterlogging which severely affects this crop.

cultivationProcess.title: Nutrient Management
cultivationProcess.description: Apply phosphorus and starter nitrogen as basal dose; rely on Rhizobium nodulation for subsequent nitrogen requirements.

cultivationProcess.title: Weed Management
cultivationProcess.description: Conduct one weeding at 15-20 days after sowing; maintain weed-free field during critical early growth period.

cultivationProcess.title: Pest and Disease Management
cultivationProcess.description: Monitor for whitefly-transmitted yellow mosaic virus closely; remove infected plants and spray insecticides at early infestation.

cultivationProcess.title: Harvesting
cultivationProcess.description: Harvest when pods turn black and dry while plants still retain some green leaves, typically 60-75 days after sowing, in 2-3 pickings.

cultivationProcess.title: Post-Harvest Handling
cultivationProcess.description: Dry harvested pods in sun, thresh to separate seeds, clean and store at 10-12% moisture in pest-proof containers.

advantages: Very short duration enables multiple cropping
High nutritional value and easy digestibility
challenges: Highly susceptible to yellow mosaic virus
storageGuidelines: Dry seeds to 10-12% moisture before storage; store in cool, dry conditions in pest-proof containers to prevent bruchid beetle infestation.
